description: One side: Testimony of Efrayim b. Ṣibyān ha-Kohen. Dating: 11th century. Describing how Yaʿqūb b. Yaʿīsh traveled in the time of the unrest and famine (צרות ורעב) to the country of Aleppo and from there to Raḥba, and took with him his daughter Jalīla bt. Menashshe. He apparently died. The other side: Unclear how it is related. Includes phrases such as "all that in the court... neither you nor your mother... everything that you (pl.) left, that is what remains...," then describes several valuable garments. The location Raḥba could refer to the village in northern Lebanon or the medieval fortress town on the Euphrates (near modern-day al-Miyādīn, Syria). (Information in part from Goitein's index card.)
description_he: צד אחד: עדות של אפרים ב. צביאן הכהן, המאה ה-11. מתאר כיצד יעקב ב. יעיש נסע לאלפו וראחבה בתקופת צרות ורעב ולקח את בתו ג'לילה בת מנשה. כנראה נפטר בדרכו. הצד השני: כולל ביטויים על פריטי לבוש יקרים
